flashcards.front: What is the time complexity of basic operations in linked list stack implementation?
flashcards.back: All basic operations (push, pop, peek, isEmpty) have O(1) time complexity.

flashcards.front: Why is linked list implementation preferred over arrays for stacks and queues?
flashcards.back: Linked lists provide dynamic sizing and efficient memory usage, unlike fixed-size arrays.

flashcards.front: What pointers are needed for queue implementation using linked list?
flashcards.back: Both front and rear pointers are needed for efficient enqueue and dequeue operations.
Stack underflow occurs when trying to pop from an empty stack.

flashcards.front: What memory management consideration is crucial in linked list implementations?
flashcards.back: Proper freeing of memory when removing nodes to avoid memory leaks.
